questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a reversible reaction?
options: It can be influenced by a catalyst
It proceeds only in forward direction
Number of moles of reactants and products are equal.
questions.solution: Reversible reactions are those reaction in which there is equilibrium established at every infinitesimal step. But the rate of forward and rate of backward reaction can be influenced (or speed up) by the use of catalyst.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: At 3000 K, the equilibrium partial pressure of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> are 0.6, 0.4 and 0.2 atm respectively. <math><msub><mrow><mi>K</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>p</mi></mrow></msub></math> for the reaction, <math><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mo>⇌</mo><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mo>+</mo><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> is
questions.solution:
2CO2 ⇌ 2CO+O2

At equili. 0.6 atm 0.4 atm 0.2 atm

∴ Kp=pCO2×pO2pCO22

 =0.42(0.2)0.62=0.088
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
The yield of the product in the reaction
$$
\mathrm{A}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+2 \mathrm{~B}(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{C}(\mathrm{g})+\mathrm{Q} \mathrm{kJ}
$$
would be higher at
questions.solution:
$A_{2}(g)+2 B(g) \rightleftharpoons C(g)+Q k J$ Since, the reaction is exothermic, So, it is favoured by low temperature.
In addition, the number of moles of products is lesser than the number of moles of reactants, thus high pressure favours the forward reaction.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Equivalent amounts of $\mathrm{H}_{2}$ and $\mathrm{I}_{2}$ are heated in a closed vessel till equilibrium is obtained. If $80 \%$ of the hydrogen can be converted to $\mathrm{HI}$, the $\mathrm{Kc}$ at this temperature is
questions.solution: $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{c}}=\frac{[\mathrm{HI}]^{2}}{\left[\mathrm{H}_{2}\right]\left[\mathrm{I}_{2}\right]}=\frac{(2 \mathrm{x})^{2}}{(1-\mathrm{x})^{2}}$ Given $\mathrm{x}=80 \%=0.80$ $\therefore \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{c}}=\frac{(2 \times 0.80)^{2}}{(1-0.80)^{2}}=64$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
For the homogenous reaction,

4NH 3 (g) +  5O 2 (g)⇌4NO(g) +  6H 2 O(g)

the equilibrium constant K c has the units
questions.solution:
4NH 3 (g) +  5O 2 (g)⇌4NO(g) +  6H 2 O(g)
K c  =  NO 4 H 2 O 6 NH 3 4 O 2 5
Hence, units of K c  =  conc +1 .
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
The reaction,
$$
2 \mathrm{~A}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{B}(\mathrm{g}) \rightleftharpoons 3 \mathrm{C}(\mathrm{g})+\mathrm{D}(\mathrm{g})
$$
is begun with the concentrations of $A$ and $B$ both at an initial value of $1.00 \mathrm{M}$. When equilibrium is reached, the concentration of $D$ is measured and found to be $0.25 \mathrm{M}$. The value for the equilibrium constant for this reaction is given by the expression
questions.solution:
Thereaction-
$\quad 2 A(\mathrm{~g})+B(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 3 C(\mathrm{~g})+D(\mathrm{~g})$
Initial $\quad 1 \quad 1$
At equil $1-0.501-0.25 \quad 0.75 \quad 0$
$\mathrm{~K}=\frac{(0.75)^{3}(0.25)}{(0.50)^{2}(0.75)}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The volume percentage of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> at equilibrium in the dissociation of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>5</mn></mrow></msub></math> under a total pressure of <math><mn>1.5</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> is <math><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0.202</mn><mo>)</mo></math> ,
questions.solution:
PCl5⇌PCl3+Cl2
1-x          x        x
Total moles =1-x+x+x=1+x
k p = P PCl 3 ⋅ PCl 2 P PCl 5 = x 1+x P 1−x 1+x P x 1+x P
kP=x2P1-x2 1-x2=1
kP=x2P
x=kpP=0.2021.5=0.134=0.366
Mole ∝ volume at constant P&T
Volume percentage =mole of Cl2totalmoles×100
=0.3661.366×100=26.86%
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The relation between <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi></mrow></msub></math> and <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></mrow></msub></math> for the reaction <math> <mrow> <mtext>A</mtext><mfenced> <mtext>g</mtext> </mfenced><mtext>+B</mtext><mfenced> <mtext>g</mtext> </mfenced><munder> <mo>⇌</mo> <mrow></mrow> </munder> <mtext>C</mtext><mfenced> <mtext>g</mtext> </mfenced><mtext>+2D</mtext><mfenced> <mtext>g</mtext> </mfenced></mrow> </math> is-
questions.solution:
Δn g = (No. Moles of products- No. of moles of reactant) =1
Δn g  = 1
Kp =  K c Δn g RT
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If the reaction $\mathrm{N}_{2}+3 \mathrm{H}_{2}=2 \mathrm{NH}_{3}$ occurs at $200^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and $1000 \mathrm{~atm}$ then the graph showing the correct equilibrium yield at $400^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ is
questions.solution: Since the formation of amnonia is an exothermic reaction hence on increasing temperature, reaction will proceed in backward direction i.e. formation of $\mathrm{NH}_{3}$ decreases.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Factors affecting <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></mrow></msub></math> is -
options: Method of writing balanced equation (or stoichiometry of reaction)
Time taken by the chemical reaction
questions.solution: <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></mrow></msub></math> depends on the expression that the coefficient of reactants and products in a given balanced equation.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
NH4HS(s)⇌NH3(g)+H2S(g)
The equilibrium pressure at 25°C is 0.660 atm. What is Kp for the reaction?
questions.solution:
NH 4 HS s ⇌ NH 3 g P + H 2 S g P
Equilibrium pressure is 2P here
2P=0.660
P=0.330
Kp=PNH3.PH2S
=0.33×0.33=0.109(atm)2
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: <math> <mrow> <mtext>1</mtext><mtext> </mtext><mtext>mole</mtext></mrow> </math> of <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msub><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mo>)</mo></math> at <math> <mrow> <mtext>300</mtext><mtext> </mtext><mtext>K</mtext></mrow> </math> is kept in a closed container under <math> <mrow> <mtext>1</mtext><mtext> </mtext><mtext>atm</mtext></mrow> </math>. It is heated to <math> <mrow> <mtext>600</mtext><mtext> </mtext><mtext>K</mtext></mrow> </math> when <math> <mrow> <mtext>20%</mtext></mrow> </math> by mole of <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msub><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mo>)</mo></math> decomposes to <math><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mo>)</mo></math>. The resultant pressure is
questions.solution:
N 2 O 4 (g) ⇌ 2NO 2 (g) Initial mole 1 0 At equi. (1-0.20) mole 0.40 mole
n total  = 1.2
P total  =  n total RT V  = 2.4 atm
as P 2 P 1  =  n 2 T 2 n 1 T 1
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
If the equilibrium constants of the following equilibria
\(\mathrm{SO}_2+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_2 \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{SO}_3 \text { and } 2 \mathrm{SO}_3 \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{SO}_2+\mathrm{O}_2\)
are given by \(\mathrm{K}_1\) and \(\mathrm{K}_2\) respectively, which of the following relations is correct
questions.solution:
Hints: \(\mathrm{K}_1=\frac{\left[\mathrm{SO}_3\right]}{\left[\mathrm{SO}_2\right]\left[\mathrm{O}_2\right]^{1 / 2}}\)
\(\mathrm{K}_2=\frac{\left[\mathrm{SO}_2\right]^2\left[\mathrm{O}_2\right]}{\left[\mathrm{SO}_3\right]^2}\)
Thus \(\mathrm{K}_2=\left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{~K}_1}\right)^2\)
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: For <math><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>⇌</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">D</mi></math> , if initially the concentration of <math> <mtext>A</mtext> </math> and <math> <mtext>B</mtext> </math> are equal but at equilibrium concentration of <math> <mtext>D</mtext> </math> will be twice of that of <math> <mtext>A</mtext> </math> then what will be the equilibrium constant of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">D</mi><mo>⇌</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math> reaction ?
questions.solution:
x=2 a−x
3x=2a
x=2a3
K c x⋅x a–x a–x = 4 a 2 9× a 3 × a 3 =4
C + D⇌A + B; K c = 1 4
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: For the reaction <math><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>⇌</mo><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>+</mo><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mo>)</mo></math> . The value of <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi></mrow></msub></math> is 4. If 2 mol of <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> , 2 mol of <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> and 2 mol of HI are present in one litre container then moles of <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> present at equilibrium is:
questions.solution:
kc=4=2+x22-2x2
2=2+x2-2x
X=0.4
Mol of I2 at equilibrium =2+0.4=2.4
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The equilibrium constant for a reaction <math><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>⇋</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">D</mi></math> is <math><mn>1</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mrow><mn>10</mn></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math> at <math> <mrow> <mn>298</mn><mtext> </mtext><mtext>K</mtext></mrow> </math> and is <math> <mn>2</mn> </math> at <math> <mrow> <mn>273</mn><mtext> </mtext><mtext>K</mtext><mtext>.</mtext></mrow> </math> The chemical process resulting in the formation of <math> <mtext>C</mtext> </math> and <math> <mtext>D</mtext> </math> is:
options: There is no relationship between <math> <mi>ΔH</mi> </math> and <math> <mtext>K</mtext> </math>
questions.solution:
log K 2 K 1 = ΔH 2.303R 1 T 1 − 1 T 2

T 1 > T 2  &  K 2 > K 1

ΔH =−Ve
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
The equilibrium constant for the following reactions are given at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$
$\begin{array}{l}
2 \mathrm{~A} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{B}+\mathrm{C}, \mathrm{K}_{1}=1.0 \\
2 \mathrm{~B} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{C}+\mathrm{D}, \mathrm{K}_{2}=16 \\
2 \mathrm{C}+\mathrm{D} \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{P}, \mathrm{K}_{3}=25
\end{array}$
The equilibrium constant for the reaction $\mathrm{P} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{A}+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{~B}$ at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ is
questions.solution:
Hint:
We can manipulate the given equations as follows
$\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{~B}+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{C} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{A}, \quad \mathrm{K}_{1}^{\prime}=1$
$\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{C}+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{D} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{B}, \quad \mathrm{K}_{2}^{\prime}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{16}}$
$\mathrm{P} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{C}+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{D}, \quad \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{3}}^{\prime}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{25}}$
$\mathrm{P} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{A}+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{~B}, \quad \mathrm{~K}_{\text {final }}=1 \times \frac{1}{4} \times \frac{1}{5}=\frac{1}{20}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The initial concentration of <math> <mtext>X</mtext> </math> and <math> <mtext>Y</mtext> </math> were <math> <mtext>2</mtext> </math> and <math> <mrow> <mtext>4</mtext><mtext> </mtext><mtext>mole/L</mtext></mrow> </math> respectively. For the following equilibrium <math><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi><mo>⇌</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">Z</mi></math> which of the following relationship among equilibrium concentrations of <math> <mrow> <mtext>X,</mtext><mtext> </mtext><mtext>Y</mtext></mrow> </math> and <math> <mtext>Z</mtext> </math> is not feasible?
questions.solution:
Now X  >  Y
2-a>4-2a
a>2
which is not feasible.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
Equilibrium constant Kp for the reaction CaCO 3 ⇌CaO+ CO 2 is 0.82 atm at 7 2 7 ∘ C.
If 1 mole of CaCO3 is placed in a closed container of 20 L and heated to this temperature, what amount of CaCO3 would dissociate at equilibrium?
questions.solution:
CaCO 3 ⇌CaO +  CO 2
K p = p CO 2 =0.82 atm
0.82 atm×20 L= n CO 2 ×0.082×1000
2 0 0 1 0 0 0 = 1 5 = n CO 2
No. of moles CO2 = no. of moles of CaCO3 decomposed = 1 5 mole
Amount of CaCO3 decomposed = 1 5  × 100 = 20 g
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
The following equilibrium constants are given:

N 2 + 3 H 2 ⇌ 2 NH 3 ; K⁡ 1

N 2 + O 2 ⇌ 2 NO ; K⁡ 2

H 2 + 1 / 2 O 2 ⇌ H 2 O ; K⁡ 3

The equilibrium constant for the oxidation of 2 mole NH 3 by oxygen to give NO is
questions.solution:
N 2 +3 H 2 ⇌ 2NH 3

K 1 = NH 3 2 N 2    H 2 3                 … i

N 2 +O 2 ⇌2NO  ;   K 2 = NO 2 N 2    O 2                 … ii

H 2 +1/2   O 2 ⇌ H 2 O  ;   K 3 = H 2 O H 2    O 2 1/2                 … iii

For the reaction, 2 NH 3 + 5 2 O 2 ⇌ 2 NO + 3 H 2 O

K⁡ = NO 2 H 2 O 3 NH 3 2 O 2 5 / 2

= NO 2 N 2 O 2 × H 2 O 3 H 2 3 O 2 3 / 2 × N 2 H 2 3 NH 3 2

= K 2 × K 3 3 K 1
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
